name: Truth Before Commitment
description: Long-term intimacy requires the person to express discomfort before withdrawing.
life_effect: The relationship branch improves when truth becomes earlier and less dramatic.

name: Stability Partner Pressure
description: Romantic and family expectations may converge around stability, increasing pressure on non-linear choices.
life_effect: Career change and intimacy choices may become coupled instead of separate.

name: Caregiver Identity Risk
description: The person may be loved for being understanding while hiding the cost of that role.
life_effect: If care is not reciprocated, intimacy becomes another performance system.

name: Private Life As Fate Anchor
description: A more honest private life can stabilize the public risk of career experimentation.
life_effect: Relational safety becomes a platform for change, not a distraction from change.

name: Marriage Pressure Coupling
description: Marriage is not only romantic; it couples family expectations, money, housing, gender roles, and future mobility.
life_effect: If these variables are not separated before commitment, the relationship can become the container for every unsolved pressure.

name: Unmet Desire Turning Outward
description: When desire and recognition cannot be spoken inside the relationship, attention may turn outward.
life_effect: The external person is often a symbol of an unlived self, not only a romantic alternative.
